clean_story: Defiance without gods.  Meaning made, not found. "Existential rebellion" borrows from Camus—the refusal to accept a silent universe as an excuse for passivity. It’s like shaking your fist at a storm, not to change the weather, but to prove you’re still standing. The rebellion isn’t against rulers or laws—it’s against the idea that life’s emptiness should paralyze you. Imagine a chess player who keeps moving pieces after realizing the board has no edges. Sisyphus pushing his boulder becomes a dancer when he chooses to enjoy the climb. Victor Frankl, in Nazi death camps, wrote that those who found purpose—even in suffering—survived longer. Rebellion here isn’t rage; it’s creativity. A street artist tagging a crumbling wall. A scientist chasing answers they’ll never see. The act itself is the meaning. Three related subjects are absurd heroism, self-made ethics, and defiant joy.
